expectations.evidence: Output has dedicated sections for LCP (Largest Contentful Paint), INP (Interaction to Next Paint), and CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) with detailed sub-bullet explanations of what each metric measures and why there is no impact.

Each metric section lists relevant sub-items (e.g., LCP covers TTFB, resource load time, render-blocking resources, client-side rendering delays; INP covers event handler execution, main thread blocking, JS execution cost; CLS covers dynamic content, images without dimensions, late-loading fonts, dynamic DOM manipulation).

Provides per-file analysis for all 3 changed files with per-metric impact statements, plus a detailed metric breakdown section. Each metric concludes with 'Impact: Zero'. Final conclusion states no positive, negative, or indirect impact on LCP, INP, or CLS.

eval_feedback.overall: The without_skill run produced an exceptionally detailed analysis with per-file and per-metric breakdowns. While it lacks the structured CWV-01 through CWV-11 checklist format, the depth of analysis is thorough and covers equivalent ground.
